card.intuition: A high-performance suggestion engine that predicts the rest of a user's search query in real-time as they type, requiring microsecond-level latency.
card.analogy: A librarian who starts finishing your sentences: as soon as you say 'Al...', they instantly guess 'Learn Blazingly Fast' or 'Algorithms' because they have a list of exactly what most people ask for, sorted by popularity.
card.time_complexity: O(L) where L is prefix length (using Trie)
when_to_use: Global search engines like Google or Bing
gotchas: Real-time updates: you can't update the Trie on every single user search in real-time (too slow). You collect searches in logs, aggregate them periodically, and update the Trie in a background 'Offline' pipeline.
Data Structure: a standard Trie is good, but for autocomplete, you often store the 'Top K' frequent completions inside each Trie node itself to avoid deep traversal.

exercise.prompt: Identify the tree-based data structure most commonly used to store and search for prefixes in an autocomplete system.
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return ans.lower() == "trie"
assert check("Trie")
print("PASS")
